title: rubus thyrsoideus subsp. neomalacus
intro: Rubus thyrsoideus subsp. neomalacus is a subspecies of the bramble plant, specifically a type of blackberry bush native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing swelling, and combating infections. The health benefits of Rubus thyrsoideus subsp. neomalacus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
